interpretation: A per-class BRRC variant that restricts the small-loop-density criterion to internal/bulge loops (where obelisks have 13.1/100nt vs 6.32 in shuffles, 2.07x fold change) gives a more mechanistically specific signature than the class-blind sld. Hairpin and multiloop densities are inverted in obelisks (0.43x and 0.23x fold change respectively), so a per-class BRRC that strictly excludes hairpin/multiloop SLD from the count would be even more specific.
